primary_diagnosis.description: Malignant neoplasm of unspecified site of right female breast

policy_excerpts.excerpt_text: Trastuzumab is medically necessary when ALL: (1) HER2-positive confirmed by IHC 3+ or FISH-amplified, (2) adjuvant or neoadjuvant setting, (3) ECOG performance status 0-2, (4) baseline LVEF ≥ 50%.
Initial authorization: 6 months. Renewal: every 12 months contingent on documented response and ECHO/MUGA-confirmed LVEF ≥ 50%.

criteria.criterion: HER2-positive confirmed by IHC 3+ or FISH

criteria.evidence: Stage IIIa, completed AC chemo, post-op

necessity_assessment.summary: All four criteria met with strong evidence. Standard adjuvant trastuzumab indication.

decision.rationale: Patient meets all four coverage criteria for trastuzumab in HER2-positive breast cancer (adjuvant). HER2 IHC 3+ confirmed (criterion 1, AETNA-MED-2024-007 § Coverage Criteria). Adjuvant setting after AC chemotherapy (criterion 2). ECOG 1 (criterion 3). LVEF 62% from echocardiogram 12 days prior (criterion 4). Authorization for 6 months per AETNA-MED-2024-007 § Authorization Period.

patient_communication.summary_text: Good news. Your insurance has approved your trastuzumab treatment for the next 6 months. You'll need an echocardiogram every 12 months to renew the authorization.
next_action_steps: Schedule first trastuzumab infusion within 7 days
Confirm next ECHO date (currently scheduled for 11 months out)
Call your care coordinator with any side-effect questions
